title: Design contract-first with OpenAPI
description: A procedure to specify the contract in OpenAPI before implementation so consumers, mocks, and stubs derive from one source.
body: Use when starting a new API or a significant addition. Do not let the implementation become the de facto spec. Steps: model resources, methods, schemas, and errors in an OpenAPI document; review it with consumers; generate mocks and client or server stubs from it; keep the spec the source of truth. Validation signals include the document validating and round-tripping to generated stubs that match consumer expectations. Risk boundary: a spec that drifts from the implementation misleads clients, so generate or test the implementation against the contract.
